[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Pick+and+roll%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Pick+and+roll%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Pick+and+roll%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Pick+and+roll%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(March 2007)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(also called a **ball screen** or **screen and roll**) in [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") is an offensive play in which a player sets a [screen](/wiki/Screen_(sports) "Screen (sports)") (pick) for a teammate handling the ball and then moves toward the basket (rolls) to receive a pass. In the [NBA](/wiki/National_Basketball_Association "National Basketball Association"), the play came into vogue in the 1990s and has developed into the league's most common offensive action.[[1]](#cite_note-abrams-1) There are, however, many ways in which the defense can also counter the offensive screen.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Offensive play in basketball

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Pick_and_roll "Special:EditPage/Pick and roll") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Pick and roll"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Pick+and+roll%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Pick+and+roll%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Pick+and+roll%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Pick+and+roll%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Pick+and+roll%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Pick+and+roll%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(March 2007)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/02/Trevor_Booker%2C_Tony_Parker_and_Kirk_Hinrich.jpg/250px-Trevor_Booker%2C_Tony_Parker_and_Kirk_Hinrich.jpg)](/wiki/File:Trevor_Booker,_Tony_Parker_and_Kirk_Hinrich.jpg)

[Trevor Booker](/wiki/Trevor_Booker "Trevor Booker") sets a "screen" on [Tony Parker](/wiki/Tony_Parker "Tony Parker") for [Kirk Hinrich](/wiki/Kirk_Hinrich "Kirk Hinrich")

The **pick and roll** (also called a **ball screen** or **screen and roll**) in [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") is an offensive play in which a player sets a [screen](/wiki/Screen_(sports) "Screen (sports)") (pick) for a teammate handling the ball and then moves toward the basket (rolls) to receive a pass. In the [NBA](/wiki/National_Basketball_Association "National Basketball Association"), the play came into vogue in the 1990s and has developed into the league's most common offensive action.[[1]](#cite_note-abrams-1) There are, however, many ways in which the defense can also counter the offensive screen.

## Execution

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pick_and_roll&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Execution")]

The play (in its elementary form) involves three players. The play begins with a [defender](/wiki/Basketball_position "Basketball position") guarding a ballhandler. The ballhandler moves toward a teammate, who sets a "screen" (or "pick") by standing in the way of the defender, who is separated from the moving ballhandler. The defender is forced to choose between guarding the ballhandler or the screener. If the defender tries to guard the ballhandler, then the screener can move toward the basket (as the player defending the screener may try to trap or guard the ballhandler, giving the screener space) sometimes by a foot [pivot](/w/index.php?title=Pivot_(basketball)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Pivot (basketball) (page does not exist)") ("roll") and is now open for a [pass](/wiki/Basketball#Passing "Basketball"). If the defender chooses instead to guard the screening teammate, then the ballhandler has an open shot.[[2]](#cite_note-krause-2)[[3]](#cite_note-wissel-3) Alternatively, the ballhandler may pass the ball to an open teammate.[[1]](#cite_note-abrams-1) A well-executed pick and roll is the result of teamwork,[[3]](#cite_note-wissel-3) but many players fail by executing the play too quickly; they must ignore the defense and control their own speed.[[4]](#cite_note-rohan20120215-4)

Defending the pick and roll is a tactical challenge. The screener's defender alerts his [teammate](/wiki/Teammate "Teammate") of the screen.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Options are "switch" - defenders swap assignments, or "hedge" - the screener's defender predicts the screen and momentarily impedes the [ballhandler](/wiki/Bale_handlers "Bale handlers").[[5]](#cite_note-5) Each choice carries risks, like mismatches or gaps in defense. Success lies in [agility](/wiki/Agility "Agility"), communication, and coordination.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d1/Pick_n_roll_by_Bon_Custodio.jpg/250px-Pick_n_roll_by_Bon_Custodio.jpg)](/wiki/File:Pick_n_roll_by_Bon_Custodio.jpg)

A forward sets a "screen" on defender

The pick and roll is often employed by a shorter [guard](/wiki/Basketball_position#Back_court "Basketball position") handling the ball and a taller [forward](/wiki/Basketball_position#Frontcourt_(forwards_and_center) "Basketball position") or [center](/wiki/Center_(basketball) "Center (basketball)") setting the screen; if the taller defender switches to guard the ballhandler, then the offensive team can have favorable mismatches. The shorter guard has a speed advantage over the taller defender, while the taller forward/center has a size advantage over the shorter defender.

### Results

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pick_and_roll&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Results")]

A successful pick and roll play may result in the screener being in position to receive a pass with a clear path for an easy shot, with the chance of drawing a [foul](/wiki/Personal_foul_(basketball) "Personal foul (basketball)") as other defenders move toward the play to try to prevent [penetration](/wiki/Penetration_(basketball) "Penetration (basketball)"). It may alternately lead to the ballhandler being momentarily without a defender and thus free to pass to any open teammate, or take an [uncontested shot](/wiki/Uncontested_shot "Uncontested shot"), which greatly improves the chance of scoring, again with the chance of drawing a foul as the screened defender hurries to get back into the play.

The success of the strategy depends largely on the ballhandler, who must recognize the situation quickly and make a decision whether to take the shot, pass to the screener who is rolling (if the defender switches) or pass to another open teammate (if other defenders come to help).[[2]](#cite_note-krause-2) The screener also must recognize the open spaces of the court to roll to and be alert to receive the pass and finish the play.

## Other variations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pick_and_roll&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Other variations")]

Variations of the pick and roll are the *pick and pop* (or *pick and fade*), where the screener moves for an open [jump shot](/wiki/Jump_shot_(basketball) "Jump shot (basketball)") instead of rolling to the basket, or the *pick and slip*, where the screener fakes setting a screen before slipping behind the defender to accept the pass.

### Pick and pop

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pick_and_roll&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Pick and pop")]

The pick and pop is an offensive play that is a derivative of the classic pick and roll. Instead of rolling toward the basket, the player setting the pick moves to an open area of the court to receive a pass from the ballhandler for a jump shot ("pop").

The premise of the two plays is the same: a ballhandler uses a teammate's pick to attract the attention of two defensive players to free his teammate for a scoring opportunity. A successful pick and pop relies on a ballhandler who demands constant defensive attention and a teammate with an accurate jump shot or a [layup](/wiki/Layup "Layup") if by the rim.

## Notable uses

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pick_and_roll&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Notable uses")]

In the NBA, [John Stockton](/wiki/John_Stockton "John Stockton") and [Karl Malone](/wiki/Karl_Malone "Karl Malone") of the [Utah Jazz](/wiki/Utah_Jazz "Utah Jazz") used this play to great effect in the 1990s,[[2]](#cite_note-krause-2) leading their team to the [NBA Finals](/wiki/NBA_Finals "NBA Finals") in 1997 and 1998. Stockton, a [point guard](/wiki/Point_guard "Point guard"), was a good shooter and exceptional decision maker and Malone, a [power forward](/wiki/Power_forward_(basketball) "Power forward (basketball)"), was a great finisher. When they were teammates on the [Phoenix Suns](/wiki/Phoenix_Suns "Phoenix Suns"), [Steve Nash](/wiki/Steve_Nash "Steve Nash") and [Amar'e Stoudemire](/wiki/Amar%27e_Stoudemire "Amar'e Stoudemire") also utilized the high-screen pick and roll to great success.[[1]](#cite_note-abrams-1)[[4]](#cite_note-rohan20120215-4) The [Golden State Warriors](/wiki/Golden_State_Warriors "Golden State Warriors")' combination of point guard [Stephen Curry](/wiki/Stephen_Curry "Stephen Curry") and power forward [Draymond Green](/wiki/Draymond_Green "Draymond Green") are noted for their use of a stutter step high pick-and-roll set: Curry's long-range shooting ability often creates situations where the opposing team will attempt to double up their defenders against him. Curry will pass to Green as he rolls out of the screen, allowing Green to lead a 4-on-3 offense with the option to pass to wing shooters, lob an [alley-oop](/wiki/Alley-oop "Alley-oop") or drive to the basket.[[6]](#cite_note-6) According to [Synergy Sports Technology](/wiki/Synergy_Sports_Technology "Synergy Sports Technology"), use of the pick and roll in the NBA rose from 15.6% of total plays in the [2004–05 NBA season](/wiki/2004%E2%80%9305_NBA_season "2004–05 NBA season") to 18.6% in the [2008–09 NBA season](/wiki/2008%E2%80%9309_NBA_season "2008–09 NBA season").[[1]](#cite_note-abrams-1) Another avid user of this move was [Chris Paul](/wiki/Chris_Paul "Chris Paul"), who was known for completing this move with his teammate [DeAndre Jordan](/wiki/DeAndre_Jordan "DeAndre Jordan") on the [Los Angeles Clippers](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Clippers "Los Angeles Clippers") from 2011-2017.

Some of the most effective pick and roll duets in the [Euroleague](/wiki/Euroleague "Euroleague") have been [Dimitris Diamantidis](/wiki/Dimitris_Diamantidis "Dimitris Diamantidis") and [Mike Batiste](/wiki/Mike_Batiste "Mike Batiste") of [Panathinaikos B.C.](/wiki/Panathinaikos_B.C. "Panathinaikos B.C."),[[7]](#cite_note-7) [Kostas Sloukas](/wiki/Kostas_Sloukas "Kostas Sloukas") and [Jan Veselý](/wiki/Jan_Vesel%C3%BD "Jan Veselý") of [Fenerbahçe İstanbul](/wiki/Fenerbah%C3%A7e_S.K._(basketball) "Fenerbahçe S.K. (basketball)") and [Sergio Rodriguez](/wiki/Sergio_Rodriguez "Sergio Rodriguez") and [Kyle Hines](/wiki/Kyle_Hines "Kyle Hines") of [CSKA Moscow](/wiki/PBC_CSKA_Moscow "PBC CSKA Moscow").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Other sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pick_and_roll&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Other sports")]

The pick and roll was also used extensively in [box lacrosse](/wiki/Box_lacrosse "Box lacrosse"), the sport played in the [National Lacrosse League](/wiki/National_Lacrosse_League "National Lacrosse League").

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pick_and_roll&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: See also")]
